summary:
The patient’s obstructive spirometry results (FEV1/FVC ≤70%, FEV1 at 50% pred.), hyperinflated lungs (increased RV), and reduced diffusing capacity are characteristic of chronic obstructive pulmonary disease. Despite past occupational exposure, absent features such as restriction, interstitial infiltrates, or chronic infection make alternative conditions less likely.  

End summary.
